# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

# app/mailers/application_mailer.rb
class ApplicationMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com")
  layout "mailer"
end

# app/mailers/sendgrid_mailer.rb
require "sendgrid-ruby"

class SendgridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  include SendGrid

  default from: ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com")

  def notify(to:, subject:, html_content:, text_content: nil, from: nil, categories: [], custom_args: {})
    @to = to
    @subject = subject
    @html_content = html_content
    @text_content = text_content
    @from = from || self.class.default[:from]
    @categories = categories
    @custom_args = custom_args

    deliver_with_sendgrid
  end

  private

  def deliver_with_sendgrid
    mail = Mail.new
    mail.from = Email.new(email: @from)
    mail.subject = @subject

    Array(@to).each do |recipient|
      personalization = Personalization.new
      personalization.add_to(Email.new(email: recipient))

      @custom_args.each do |key, value|
        personalization.add_custom_arg(CustomArg.new(key: key.to_s, value: value.to_s))
      end

      mail.add_personalization(personalization)
    end

    mail.add_content(Content.new(type: "text/plain", value: @text_content)) if @text_content.present?
    mail.add_content(Content.new(type: "text/html", value: @html_content)) if @html_content.present?

    Array(@categories).each do |category|
      mail.add_category(Category.new(name: category.to_s))
    end

    response = sendgrid_client.client.mail._("send").post(request_body: mail.to_json)

    unless response.status_code.to_i.between?(200, 299)
      Rails.logger.error("SendGrid delivery failed: #{response.status_code} #{response.body}")
      raise "SendGrid delivery failed with status #{response.status_code}"
    end

    response
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error("SendGrid mailer error: #{e.class} - #{e.message}")
    raise
  end

  def sendgrid_client
    api_key = ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"]
    raise "Missing SENDGRID_API_KEY" if api_key.blank?

    SendGrid::API.new(api_key: api_key)
  end
end

# config/initializers/sendgrid.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = if Rails.env.production?
    {
      host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST"),
      protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "https")
    }
  else
    {
      host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
      port: ENV.fetch("APP_PORT", 3000),
      protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "http")
    }
  end

  config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true

  if Rails.env.production?
    config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
    config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
      address: "smtp.sendgrid.net",
      port: 587,
      domain: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST"),
      user_name: "apikey",
      password: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_API_KEY"),
      authentication: :plain,
      enable_starttls_auto: true
    }
  else
    config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
    config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
      address: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_ADDRESS", "smtp.sendgrid.net"),
      port: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_PORT", 587).to_i,
      domain: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
      user_name: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_USERNAME", "apikey"),
      password: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_API_KEY"),
      authentication: :plain,
      enable_starttls_auto: true
    }
  end
end

# config/environments/development.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = {
    host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
    port: ENV.fetch("APP_PORT", 3000),
    protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "http")
  }
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
  config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
    address: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_ADDRESS", "smtp.sendgrid.net"),
    port: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_PORT", 587).to_i,
    domain: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
    user_name: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_USERNAME", "apikey"),
    password: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_API_KEY"),
    authentication: :plain,
    enable_starttls_auto: true
  }
end

# config/environments/production.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = {
    host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST"),
    protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "https")
  }
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
  config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
    address: "smtp.sendgrid.net",
    port: 587,
    domain: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST"),
    user_name: "apikey",
    password: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_API_KEY"),
    authentication: :plain,
    enable_starttls_auto: true
  }
end

# Example usage:
# SendgridMailer.new.notify(
#   to: "user@example.com",
#   subject: "Welcome",
#   html_content: "<strong>Hello from Rails + SendGrid</strong>",
#   text_content: "Hello from Rails + SendGrid"
# )
